name: Auntie Yuan Duck Salad
recipeInstructions:
----------------------------------DRESSING----------------------------------
1 teaspoon Mustard (dry-fried)
3½ ounces Rice sticks (Mai fun)
2 tablespoons Lettuce, shredded (iceberg)
6 Scallions, thinly sliced
1 tablespoon Soy Sauce
1/3 cup Chicken stock (see recipe note)
1/3 cup Chinese rice vinegar
1/3 cup Vegetable oil (for deep-frying)
Sesame seeds for garnish
For Roast Duck:
Preheat oven to 400°F. Rub the duck cavity with soy sauce, salt, and peppercorns. Place on a rack in roasting pan.
Mix honey and vinegar; brush over duck. Roast until crispy and golden, about 1 hour, brushing occasionally.
Remove skin from duck breasts and cut into thin slices. Remove meat from duck legs and slice thinly. Combine the pieces (reserve 1 cup).
For Dressing:
Combine all dressing ingredients in a bowl; set aside.
Cooking:
Heat oil to 450°F in wok or wide casserole. Add rice sticks for a few seconds until they puff, then turn carefully.
Drain noodles on paper towels. Break up and divide among plates.
Cover with lettuce and reserved duck slices. Garnish with scallions and cilantro.
Dress salad lightly and drizzle over each serving; sprinkle sesame seeds before serving.
